title: Meeting People
description: Introduce yourself, ask names, and start a friendly conversation in Filipino.
situation: First introductions: names, where you are from, and the warm small talk Filipinos love.

phrases.tip: Ninyo is the polite you; with friends use mo: ano ang pangalan mo?
Use si before a person's name, never ang.
Shorter and very common: ikinagagalak ko po.
Swap in any place: taga-Maynila ako (I'm from Manila).
A normal, friendly question here, not rude.
Family questions are a sign of warmth, not nosiness.

phrases.native: Magkano ang edad mo? Biro lang!
phrases.english: How old are you? Just kidding!
phrases.pronunciation: mag-KA-no ang e-DAD mo, BEE-ro lang
phrases.tip: Biro lang (just joking) keeps things light and friendly.
Tayo means us, including the listener.

phrases.native: Pwede ba kitang i-add sa Facebook?
phrases.english: Can I add you on Facebook?
phrases.pronunciation: PWE-de ba KEE-tang ee-AD sa fays-BUK
phrases.tip: Facebook is hugely popular, so this is a very natural ask.
